metadata.title: GardenOS — Smart garden & robot mowing
metadata.description: mygardenos.com: digitized gardens and robot mowing subscriptions.

home.headline: Your garden, on autopilot
home.sub: GardenOS handles the mapping, monitoring, and mowing—so your yard looks great without stealing your weekend.

col1.title: Know everything about your garden in one place
col1.body: See boundaries, key areas, and what’s happening over time—so decisions are simple and surprises are rare.

col2.title: Mowing that runs on a schedule, not your weekend
col2.body: Set it and forget it. Regular mowing keeps things tidy without the last‑minute scramble before guests arrive.

col3.title: Works for one yard or twenty
col3.body: Whether it’s your home or multiple properties, you get the same consistent setup, visibility, and results.

prototype.title: Next test window opening soon — be the first to know
prototype.body: We run live mowing passes, boundary checks, and edge‑case drills. Register for a reminder and we'll send you the details.

registerBlock.title: Join early and lock in your subscription perks
registerBlock.body: Drop your email to get product updates, pilot spots, and early pricing.

about.metaDescription: Mission, vision, problems we solve, and values behind GardenOS digitized gardens and subscription mowing.
about.title: About GardenOS
about.origin: GardenOS is a robotic lawn mowing subscription — we map your yard, run the mowing on a schedule, and manage the whole thing remotely. GardenOS started with a simple problem: keeping up with lawn growth shouldn’t take hours every week. We’re a small team in Auckland building a calmer, more reliable way to take care of outdoor spaces—starting at home, then scaling out.
about.lead: We focus on the real-world pain: unclear boundaries, unpredictable results, and mowing that eats weekends. Our approach is practical—map the yard, monitor what matters, and run mowing as a repeatable service you can trust.

mission.title: Mission
mission.body: Make reliable lawn care ridiculously easy: use 3D mapping + robotics to deliver consistent results, with subscription pricing that’s built for ultimate value.

vision.title: Vision
vision.body: Help more people enjoy a yard they’re proud of—without the constant time and stress. As we learn in the field, we’ll make outdoor care simpler, more predictable, and more affordable.

p1.title: Fragmented knowledge, hero-dependent ops
p1.body: Boundaries, slopes, vegetation, and fleet health live across paper trails and chats—hard to audit or hand off.

p2.title: Mowing is costly to scale
p2.body: NZ spring growth is relentless—crews fluctuate, and robots idle without disciplined ops. We close the loop with mapping, scheduling, and remote operations that are repeatable and measurable.

p3.title: Subscriptions need a trustworthy backbone
p3.body: Moving from one-off sales to recurring care demands transparent job logs, predictable windows, and accountable remote operations.

v1.title: Safety and compliance first
v1.body: Outdoor robotics must respect boundaries, privacy, and local rules before feature velocity.

v2.title: Data should serve the owner
v2.body: Mapping and mowing telemetry stay within the scope you authorize for optimization—not a lock-in ransom.

v3.title: Co-build with the community
v3.body: Early users, property partners, and builders shape the roadmap with grounded field feedback.

rel1.title: v0.4 — Subscription billing preview
rel1.body: Draft pricing by parcel size and cadence with exportable reconciliation reports (test data).

rel2.title: v0.3 — Boundary regression toolkit
rel2.body: Compare remapping deltas and auto-generate review task lists.

rel3.title: v0.2 — Remote ops console
rel3.body: Searchable job logs, explainable fault codes, and remote safe-stop controls.

cta.title: Exploring pilots or partnerships?
cta.body: Start with how to participate, or create a membership to get updates and invites.

participate.metaDescription: Three ways to engage, baseline requirements, and sample cadence for GardenOS pilots and co-creation.
participate.title: Join the GardenOS early build
participate.lead: Homeowners, property operators, and technical partners can all contribute—the paths below turn your signal into roadmap decisions.

step1.title: Try it out
step1.body: Get early access to the experience and see how it feels in a real yard. You’ll get guidance and updates as we iterate.

step2.title: Give feedback
step2.body: Tell us what matters in your situation—your yard, your schedule, your constraints. We’ll turn that into clearer priorities and better product decisions.

step3.title: Run a pilot
step3.body: Work with us on a real pilot so you can evaluate results over time. You’ll get structured check-ins, reporting, and a clear plan for next steps.

participate.reqIntro: We keep participation lightweight and respectful of your time. A few helpful basics make the experience smoother for everyone.
participate.req1: We’ll confirm what access (if any) is needed for mapping and testing before anything happens.
participate.req2: If your site has special constraints (gates, steep slopes, complex edges), tell us early so we plan around them.
participate.req3: Feedback can be as simple as “what worked / what didn’t.” If you have time, screenshots and short notes help a lot.
participate.req4: Dates can shift as we test; members get the fastest updates when schedules are confirmed.
participate.scheduleBody: Q2: prototype revisit + boundary regression week. Q3: expand pilot metros and billing dry-runs. Official dates publish on Live & testing.
participate.ctaBody: RSVP for the next public test, or create a membership to hold launch perks and notifications.

live.metaDescription: Public prototype windows, how to watch, replays, and release notes for GardenOS field tests.
live.title: Live tests and how to join
live.lead: We run open prototype tests on a rolling schedule. Register once, and we’ll notify you the moment a date is confirmed.
live.windowBody: No date locked yet. When the next session is confirmed, we’ll share the RSVP link, what we’re testing, and how to watch.
live.windowNote: If you register, you’ll get notified when it’s official. If you can’t make it live, we’ll post the recap and replay link here.
live.channelsTitle: How to watch and download assets

ch1.title: Live broadcast
ch1.body: Public sessions include multi-camera streams and captions—join five minutes early for the safety briefing.

ch2.title: Replays & write-ups
ch2.body: Edited replays and written recaps ship within 48 hours, including parameter ranges and known limitations.

ch3.title: Technical briefs
ch3.body: Each release ships with a changelog and migration notes; breaking behavior is called out explicitly.

rel1.title: v0.4 — Subscription billing preview
rel1.body: Draft pricing by parcel size and cadence with exportable reconciliation reports (test data).

rel2.title: v0.3 — Boundary regression toolkit
rel2.body: Compare remapping deltas and auto-generate review task lists.

rel3.title: v0.2 — Remote ops console
rel3.body: Searchable job logs, explainable fault codes, and remote safe-stop controls.

live.bannerBody: Create a membership and enable email alerts to get RSVP and watch links the moment a schedule drops.

register.metaDescription: Register for GardenOS updates, pilot invites, and test schedules.
register.title: Create your GardenOS membership
register.lead: Register once to get updates, pilot invites, and notifications when live test dates are confirmed.

b1.title: Product updates & roadmap
b1.body: Regular digests on shipped features, known issues, and upcoming milestones.

b2.title: Pilot and event alerts
b2.body: Get first notice when cohorts open; some sessions are members-only.

b3.title: Launch and subscription conversations
b3.body: During pricing dry-runs, join surveys or interviews for a shot at early bands or service bundles (per published rules).

register.formSideBody: We’ll only use this to send updates you asked for: test dates, pilot openings, and important product milestones.
register.formHint: Fill this out and we’ll keep you in the loop.
register.submitError: We could not submit your registration right now. Please try again.
register.submitMissingConfig: Registration is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.
register.reassurance: No spam. Just updates when it matters.
register.sideBody: Skim About and How to participate before choosing a track.
